title: Orana Wildlife Park Review
meta_desc: Discover NZ's only open-range zoo, perfect for families with kids of all ages. Get insider tips, prices, and how to save money at Orana Wildlife Park in Christchurch.
intro: Orana Wildlife Park in Christchurch is a must-visit for families, offering an unforgettable experience with its open-range zoo and native wildlife section. With prices at $32 per adult and $14 per child, it's an affordable day out. The park is suitable for children of all ages, making it an ideal destination for families.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Orana Wildlife Park is truly one of a kind, providing an immersive experience as you ride through the African Savannah enclosure by bus, surrounded by lions, giraffes, and rhinos. The highlight for many kids is the opportunity to hand-feed the giraffes at 2 pm daily. The native wildlife section is also excellent, showcasing the best of New Zealand's flora and fauna.

The duration of the visit can range from 3 to 4 hours, depending on how much time you spend exploring the various enclosures and exhibits. It's worth noting that the park is stroller-friendly, making it accessible for families with younger children.
list: Ride through the African Savannah enclosure

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
Given the unique experiences offered, Orana Wildlife Park provides good value for money. To make the most of your visit, consider arriving early to beat the crowds and ensure you catch the giraffe feeding session. If you're looking to save money, keep an eye on the park's website for special promotions or package deals.

It's also worth considering the cost of food and drinks within the park, as you may want to budget for these extras. Bringing your own picnic can be a cost-effective option, but there are also cafes and kiosks available on site.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and Booking
sections.body: The best time to visit Orana Wildlife Park is during the week or outside of school holidays to avoid the crowds. Booking your tickets in advance is recommended, especially during peak season, to ensure availability. You can book tickets directly through the Orana Wildlife Park website or at the gate on the day of your visit.
list: Book tickets in advance for peak season
Visit during the week for smaller crowds
Check the website for special promotions

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body:
While Orana Wildlife Park is generally an excellent choice for families, there are a few downsides to consider. The park can get quite crowded during peak season, which may test the patience of younger children. Additionally, some of the animal enclosures may be a distance apart, requiring a bit of walking.

However, these minor drawbacks are far outweighed by the unique experiences and opportunities for learning and fun that the park provides. With some planning and preparation, families can have a truly enjoyable and memorable day out at Orana Wildlife Park.
